hero.title: 5 Days, Golden Triangle.
hero.intro: The same iconic Delhi, Agra and Jaipur route, designed for Muslim travellers. Halal meals, prayer time built in, and visits to the great Mughal mosques and monuments that tell the story of Islamic India.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Delhi Agra Jaipur + Taj Mahal (Muslim Friendly)

overview.heading: 5 Days Delhi Agra Jaipur + Taj Mahal (Muslim Friendly): The Full Picture
body: This is the most popular India trip for Muslim travellers from Malaysia: the classic Golden Triangle of Delhi, Agra and Jaipur, arranged with halal meals and a Muslim-friendly itinerary throughout. India's Mughal heritage means the route is rich with grand mosques, tombs and monuments built by Islamic emperors.

This 5 day Muslim-friendly Golden Triangle package keeps everything private, with your own vehicle and driver guide. Halal food is arranged at each stop, prayer times are respected, and you visit landmarks like Jama Masjid, the Taj Mahal (itself a Mughal mausoleum) and Agra Fort, all built during the height of Mughal India.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Taj Mahal, a Mughal Masterpiece
items.body: The Taj Mahal was built by Emperor Shah Jahan as a tomb for his wife. Seeing it at sunrise, then understanding its place in Islamic and Mughal history, is the heart of this trip.

items.title: Jama Masjid & Mughal Delhi
items.body: Delhi's Jama Masjid is one of the largest mosques in India, built by Shah Jahan. Combined with Humayun's Tomb and the Red Fort, it tells the story of the Mughal capital.

items.title: Halal Dining & Comfortable Stays
items.body: Halal meals are arranged throughout the trip, from Mughlai cuisine in Delhi to Rajasthani-style halal fare in Jaipur, with prayer time respected in the daily schedule.

days.title: Arrive Delhi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free, with a halal dinner arranged nearby.
suggestedActivities: Optional evening prayer at a local mosque

days.title: Mughal Delhi, Drive to Agra
days.description: Jama Masjid, the Red Fort and Humayun's Tomb, then drive to Agra.

days.title: Taj Mahal Sunrise, Agra Fort, Drive to Jaipur
days.description: Sunrise at the Taj, then Agra Fort, before driving to Jaipur via Fatehpur Sikri.

days.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
days.description: A full day in the Pink City with halal meals arranged.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Muslim-Friendly India, Done Properly
whyBook.body: Arranging halal meals and respecting prayer times across three cities takes local knowledge and the right contacts. We have built Muslim-friendly itineraries for Malaysian travellers for many years. Dhesu has served Malaysia since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Muslim-Friendly Golden Triangle from Malaysia
sections.body:
For Muslim travellers from Malaysia, the Golden Triangle is a natural choice. India's Mughal heritage means the route is full of magnificent Islamic architecture, and the Taj Mahal itself is a Mughal mausoleum.

This package arranges halal meals throughout and respects prayer times, so you can enjoy Delhi, Agra and Jaipur with peace of mind. Your private vehicle and guide keep everything flexible.

Halal food is widely available across North India, and we work with trusted restaurants and arrangements at every stop.

sections.heading: Halal Food and Prayer on the Trip
sections.body:
Halal meals are arranged at each city, from Delhi's famous Mughlai cuisine to halal options in Agra and Jaipur. Mosques are plentiful across North India, and your driver guide can point you to the nearest for prayer times.

The Jama Masjid in Delhi and the great mosque at Fatehpur Sikri are both part of the itinerary, combining sightseeing with places of worship.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (DEL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Are halal meals included throughout?
items.answer: Yes. This package is designed for Muslim travellers, with halal meals arranged at each stop across Delhi, Agra and Jaipur. Breakfast is included daily, and we arrange halal options for other meals on request.

items.question: Is prayer time accommodated in the itinerary?
items.answer: Yes. The schedule respects prayer times, and your driver guide can direct you to the nearest mosque. North India has abundant mosques, including the historic Jama Masjid in Delhi.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: October to March is ideal, with comfortable weather for sightseeing. April to June is very hot, and July to September is the monsoon. Winter offers the best conditions for the Taj at sunrise.
